page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an AMD EPYC 9T25 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. With a 1:1 ratio of vCPUs to physical cores, this instance provides dedicated CPU allocation, ensuring consistent compute performance without resource contention from hyperthreading.

The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This hardware profile is tailored for compute-heavy workloads that require substantial processing power but do not demand massive memory footprints. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning storage requirements must be met via external network-attached block storage.

Given its dedicated AMD EPYC processor cores and compute-focused memory ratio, the ecs.c9a.8xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high CPU density and predictable execution times. Appropriate workloads include batch processing, scientific modeling, and distributed analytics where compute performance is the primary bottleneck.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9T25 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, running at 2.7 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It offers a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps, with no local storage or integrated GPUs. This hardware profile delivers consistent, dedicated compute performance, making it appropriate for CPU-bound workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and distributed compute tasks.
